name: Ascent to La Morra and Las Majadillas via the Sierra de San Pablo — circular from Puerto del Robledillo
name_original: Subida a La Morra y a Las Majadillas por la Sierra de San Pablo — circular desde Puerto del Robledillo
region_intro: Hiking around San Pablo De Los Montes offers access to a diverse natural environment within the Montes de Toledo mountain range. The landscape features well-preserved Mediterranean forests, characterized by oak, holm oak, and cork trees, alongside high cliffs and streams. This region represents the highest altitudes of the Montes de Toledo, contributing to varied terrain including scree slopes and areas with crystalline waters. Best hiking trails around San Pablo De Los Montes The most popular hiking route is La Morra and El Cerillón Loop, a 6.6 miles (10.7 km) moderate trail that takes 3 hours 24 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the surrounding Mediterranean forest and varied terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Las Majadillas and Peña Gorda Loop, a moderate 5.4 miles (8.7 km) path. This trail winds through forested areas and offers glimpses of the region's characteristic rock formations. Local hikers also love the La Morra and Cerillón Loop, a 9.1 miles (14.6 km) trail leading through Mediterranean forest and elevated sections, often completed in about 4 hours 33 minutes. Hiking around San Pablo De Los Montes is defined by its Mediterranean forest ecosystems, the Montes de Toledo mountain range, and areas with crystalline waters. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in San Pablo De Los Montes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 80 hikers have used komoot to explore San Pablo De Los Montes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around San Pablo De Los Montes?
A: There are nearly 20 hiking trails around San Pablo De Los Montes, offering a range of experiences from easy strolls to more challenging routes. The area is well-regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hiking trails in San Pablo De Los Montes?
A: The terrain around San Pablo De Los Montes is diverse, characterized by well-preserved Mediterranean forests of oak, holm oak, and cork. You'll encounter high cliffs, picturesque streams, and varied landscapes including 'canchales' (scree slopes) due to its location in the Montes de Toledo mountain range. Trails often feature crystalline waters and abundant springs.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in San Pablo De Los Montes?
A: Yes, San Pablo De Los Montes offers several trails suitable for families. While specific 'easy' routes are available, many moderate trails can be enjoyed by active families. The region's natural beauty, with its diverse flora and fauna, provides an engaging environment for all ages.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes in San Pablo De Los Montes are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1020945007/la-morra-and-el-cerillon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Morra and El Cerillón Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular trail covering about 10.7 km. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1198313950/las-majadillas-and-canada-real-segoviana-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Majadillas and Cañada Real Segoviana Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 6.2 km circular path.

Q: What natural features or wildlife might I encounter while hiking?
A: Hikers can expect to see extensive Mediterranean forests, crystalline streams, and diverse wildlife. The area is home to deer, roe deer, wild boars, and various birds of prey like the imperial eagle and griffon vulture. Rare species such as the Iberian lynx and amphibians like newts and salamanders can also be found. The flora includes holm oaks, cork oaks, heather, and rosemary.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or historical sites to see along the trails?
A: The region is rich in historical sites. You can discover the ruins of Roman mines from the 2nd century BCE, remains of a 15th-century Augustinian Convent, and an impressive stone snow well. Other points of interest include the 'Cruz del Siglo' and traditional 'chozos' (shepherds' huts). For panoramic views, visit the Ermita de la Fuente Santa.

Q: Are there any trails with significant viewpoints?
A: Yes, the area offers several scenic viewpoints. The Ermita de la Fuente Santa, also known as the Virgen de Gracias hermitage, provides breathtaking panoramic views of the surrounding landscape. The Robledillo Pass is also noted for its beautiful vistas.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in San Pablo De Los Montes?
A: The region's streams and natural features are especially vibrant in spring, making it an excellent time to visit. The clean, fresh air and blue skies create an inviting atmosphere for exploring the natural surroundings throughout much of the year, though summers can be hot.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by water bodies like reservoirs or lakes?
A: Yes, you can find trails near water bodies. The Torcón Reservoir and Laguna del Prao de la Casa are nearby, offering perfect settings for walking, fishing, and birdwatching. While not directly on all hiking routes, they are easily accessible and provide beautiful natural environments.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in San Pablo De Los Montes?
A: The trails in San Pablo De Los Montes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 15 reviews. Hikers often praise the well-preserved Mediterranean forest, the diverse terrain, and the sense of tranquility found in this part of the Montes de Toledo.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes available?
A: For those seeking a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1198355508/menasalbas-necropolis-trail-pr-to-36" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Menasalbas-Necropolis Trail (PR-TO 36)&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 18.1 km route. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1020949286/la-morra-and-cerillon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Morra and Cerillón Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 14.6 km with significant elevation changes, typically taking over 4 hours to complete.

Q: Is San Pablo De Los Montes close to any larger natural parks?
A: Yes, San Pablo De Los Montes is conveniently located very close to Cabañeros National Park. This proximity further expands the options for exploring unspoiled natural environments and extensive hiking trails, making it an ideal base for nature enthusiasts.
tips: Starting point for several trails, for example, to go up to Las Majadillas.
A comfortable and wide path that leads to La Morra peak (1366 m). Great views of the Montes de Toledo and the Torre de Abraham Reservoir.
A few kilometers from the town of San Pablo de los Montes is the La Morra hill. With its peak at 1,366 meters above sea level, it offers beautiful views of the entire Toledo steppe.

Summit of 1328 m, a few kilometers from the town of San Pedro de los Montes. To get there you can choose one of several trails that are around.
The views from the top of the summit will not disappoint you - you will be able to recognize several villages and even the Torre de Abraham reservoir.
